Patch Set 3:

Jeremy, I'm a bit confused. In patch set 2, it seems like you removed the cp parameter from kiblnd_create_peer() and always set it to the default value. You only over write these values in passive connect in case we encounter the race condition, but wouldn't you want to set the values in the cp in the peer in the normal case and in the race condition? I can see that we can remove the cp parameter completely from kiblnd_create_peer(), but at the same time we should be setting it after the peer is determined in passive_connect().

Am I missing something?

Patch Set 3:

ok... I was about to respond to your previous comment, but now I don't have to :) I'm good with the rest of the patch.

Patch Set 2:

I probably should have mentioned when I posted on the bug I was going to create a patch but since you already took care of it I've just added a few changes.  In my testing with 40 machines using random values for peer_credits and map_on_demand they have all successfully connected to each other.  I've also added some code to prevent the null dereference in LU-7314.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 3: -Code-Review

In patch set #2 the ibp_queue_depth/ibp_max_frags are set in three places: 1. in the race found in kiblnd_passive_connect(), 2. kiblnd_reconnect(), and 3. in kiblnd_create_peer().   If it wasn't one of the first two conditions the peer accepted our own connection parameters which were the defaults now set in kiblnd_create_peer().  So we don't really have to update them.  The kiblnd_rejected->kiblnd_reconnect path will take care of the necessary updates due to a mismatch.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Was there something wrong with patchset 2?  In ibc_queue_depth/ibc_max_frags are never set now.

Patch Set 3:

Amir, I see what you are referring to now with the non-race case in kiblnd_passive_connect().  The peer should update the settings based off the connection params there as well.  I will change the patch and resubmit.  Do you have any other concerns with patch set #2?

parents.subject: LU-7324 lnet: recv could access freed message

commit.subject: LU-3322 lnet: make connect parameters persistent
commit.message:
LU-3322 lnet: make connect parameters persistent

Store map-on-demand and peertx credits in the peer, since the peer
is persistent. Also made sure that when assigning the parameters
received on the connection to the peer structure through create,
that if another peer is added before grabbing the lock we assign
these parameters to it as well.

Signed-off-by: Amir Shehata <amir.shehata@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ie68f1ba1349d15b0a31eff9a2ca454df8e408ea9
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17074
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
